item.aggregated_output: Using megaplan skill for batch execution and checkpointing. I’m reading the batch-relevant files first, then I’ll patch the helper implementations in multi-drag-utils.ts.

---
name: megaplan
description: Run an idea through the megaplan planning loop with enforced sequencing and audit artifacts.
---

# Megaplan

Use this skill when the user wants a plan to go through a structured planner and critic loop before execution.

You must route every workflow step through `megaplan`. Do not call `claude` or `codex` directly for the workflow itself, because that bypasses enforcement and audit logging.

## Start

Run:

```bash
megaplan init --project-dir "$PROJECT_DIR" "$IDEA"
```

Read the JSON response and report:
- The plan name
- The current state
- The next step

## Workflow

Always follow the `next_step` or `valid_next` fields returned by the CLI.

Standard loop:
1. `megaplan plan`
2. `megaplan critique`
3. `megaplan evaluate`
4. If recommendation is `CONTINUE`, run `megaplan integrate` and loop back to critique.
5. If recommendation is `SKIP`, run `megaplan gate`.
6. If recommendation is `ESCALATE` or `ABORT`, present the result (including `suggested_override` and `override_rationale`) and use `megaplan override ...` only if the user chooses to bypass.
7. After a successful gate, run `megaplan execute --confirm-destructive`.
8. Finish with `megaplan review`.

If the user says "run autonomously" or similar, proceed through steps without
waiting for confirmation at each checkpoint. Still report results at the end.

## Session Defaults

Both agents default to **persistent** sessions. The Codex critic carries context
across iterations so it can verify its own prior feedback was addressed.

Override flags (mutually exclusive):
- `--fresh` — start a new persistent session (break continuity)
- `--ephemeral` — truly one-off call with no session saved
- `--persist` — explicit persistent (default; mainly for Claude review override)

## Reporting

After each step, summarize:
- What happened from `summary`
- Which artifacts were written
- What the next step is

If the user wants detail, inspect the artifact files under `.megaplan/plans/<plan-name>/`.

## Useful Commands

```bash
megaplan status --plan <name>
megaplan audit --plan <name>
megaplan list
megaplan override add-note --plan <name> "user context"
megaplan override abort --plan <name>
```

item.text: The initial scan points to this being verification-only so far: `computeDropPosition` is driven by row geometry and source kind, `DropIndicator` already keys the new-track affordance off `isNewTrack && trackId === undefined`, and the ghost code still intentionally falls back to the source row when no real target row exists yet. I’m reading the exact snippets now to confirm there isn’t a hidden edge-case that needs a patch.

The verification stayed on the “no patch needed” path. I’m running the existing drag-related test modules and a type check now so batch 3 has an explicit verification trail instead of only code inspection notes.

item.text: The full video-editor suite is green: 24 files, 89 tests. I’m writing the batch checkpoint now with the new integration coverage, the repro-script result, and the explicit confirmation that the single-clip and existing-track multi-drag paths still passed in the full regression.

I also inspected `useClipDrag.ts` to confirm the single-clip new-track branch at lines 365-377 remains untouched and the existing-track multi-drag rows path still lives in the fallback at lines 419-440. Final verification passed with the full video-editor regression suite.","files_changed":["src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipDrag.test.tsx"],"commands_run":["npx eslint src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipDrag.test.tsx","npx vitest run src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipDrag.test.tsx","npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false","node --import tsx /tmp/multi-clip-new-track-repro.ts","npx vitest run src/tools/video-editor/"]}],"sense_check_acknowledgments":[{"sense_check_id":"SC6","executor_note":"Confirmed by the final full regression run that all existing video-editor tests passed after the change set: `npx vitest run src/tools/video-editor/` completed with 24 passed test files and 89 passed tests, including the single-clip drag path, same-track multi-drag, cross-track multi-drag to existing tracks, and the new multi-clip new-track integration case."}]}

items.text: Inspect and extend useClipDrag.test.tsx to cover a multi-clip drop onto a new track that commits via type:'config'.
Run a temporary repro script for the multi-clip new-track scenario, confirm the fix, and delete the script.
Run the full video-editor vitest suite, address failures if any, and write the T6 checkpoint.
